message:
fix(security): add NaN/Inf guards to wilson_interval and successes>total check (bd-1bys)

wilson_interval() could theoretically produce NaN if successes > total (causing
negative radicand in sqrt). While current callers prevent this via
saturating_sub, the guard is defense-in-depth. Also added is_finite() check
on center/radius outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1b9x","title":"[10.21] Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Why This Exists:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Task Objective:
Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Hazard outputs are calibrated and monotonic under defined risk assumptions; censoring handling and covariate drift strategy are explicit and test-covered.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/bpet/hazard_model.rs`, `docs/specs/bpet_time_to_compromise_model.md`, `artifacts/10.21/bpet_hazard_calibration_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-1b9x/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-1b9x/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.21] Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.21] Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.21] Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.21] Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.21] Implement survival/hazard model for compromise-propensity progression under observed trajectory patterns." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Hazard outputs are calibrated and monotonic under defined risk assumptions; censoring handling and covariate drift strategy are explicit and test-covered.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:08.207596236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:04.335816557Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:04.335788946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-21","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b9x","depends_on_id":"bd-2lll","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:05:37.543904782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
